audience: SMB owner or buyer who wants safer AI adoption without becoming an AI tooling expert.
copy_rule: Show the proof, not the dream. Lead with the owner's stuck workflow, then show the controlled path forward.

evidence_refs.label: Generated repo trajectory
evidence_refs.owner_meaning: The message comes from work history, not a fresh pitch.

evidence_refs.label: Generated story
evidence_refs.owner_meaning: A reviewer can inspect the path from origin to current state.

evidence_refs.label: Shared story package
evidence_refs.owner_meaning: The wording and visual grammar are reusable across projects.

evidence_refs.label: Frontend quality gate
evidence_refs.owner_meaning: A page cannot pass as a generic card stack with unsupported claims.

evidence_refs.label: Component payload
evidence_refs.owner_meaning: The proof story has shared UI primitives instead of one-off copy.

quality_gates: playwright screenshot checks for desktop and mobile proof primitives

headline: Buy back the time hiding between your tools.

method_steps.visible_wording: Your business already has the data. The work is just hidden between tools.
method_steps.visual_cue: A workflow map with disconnected systems and one highlighted manual route.

method_steps.visible_wording: A slice is one bounded workflow improvement: useful enough to feel, small enough to inspect, and clear enough to stop if the proof is not there.
method_steps.visual_cue: The selected slice is pulled out of the map and placed on a workbench.

method_steps.visible_wording: If a claim is not proven, it stays blocked.
method_steps.visual_cue: A proof rail with proven, blocked, skipped-with-reason, and private states.

method_steps.visible_wording: The repo history shows the pivots, blockers, and lessons that survived contact with reality.
method_steps.visual_cue: A trajectory rail that connects foundation, friction, proof loop, reuse, and current arc.

method_steps.visible_wording: Map one workflow before sending secrets, customer data, or system access.
method_steps.visual_cue: A safe intake room with redacted examples and a direct operator route.

owner_problem: The business already has systems. The waste sits between them: copying, chasing, checking, remembering, and wondering what changed.

page_rooms.owner_job: Make the owner feel the trapped work between tools before naming AI.
page_rooms.visible_scene: A first-room operating map with existing tools, owner memory, and one highlighted manual route.

page_rooms.owner_job: Name the ten reasons SMB owners hesitate and show the control beside each one.
page_rooms.visible_scene: A visible friction band where unsupported claims are held in amber until the proof catches up.

page_rooms.owner_job: Define a slice as the safe unit of work: useful, inspectable, stoppable.
page_rooms.visible_scene: A cinematic but calm buyer journey: workflow room, slice workbench, proof theater, trajectory rail, contact room.

page_rooms.owner_job: Show proven, blocked, skipped, and private states without forcing raw receipt reading.
page_rooms.visible_scene: A proof rail beside the workflow with proven, blocked, skipped-with-reason, and private states.

page_rooms.owner_job: Convert git history into origin, friction, proof loop, reuse, and current arc.
page_rooms.visible_scene: A cinematic but calm buyer journey: workflow room, slice workbench, proof theater, trajectory rail, contact room.

page_rooms.owner_job: Show which lessons became shared checks, copy, tokens, components, or runbooks.
page_rooms.visible_scene: A lesson ledger showing which checks, words, components, or runbooks were created from the last hard-earned lesson.

page_rooms.owner_job: Let technical reviewers inspect artifacts after the owner story lands.
page_rooms.visible_scene: A proof rail beside the workflow with proven, blocked, skipped-with-reason, and private states.

page_rooms.owner_job: Ask for a redacted workflow example, not secrets or broad access.
page_rooms.visible_scene: A cinematic but calm buyer journey: workflow room, slice workbench, proof theater, trajectory rail, contact room.

proof_lanes.owner_meaning: The story comes from the work that actually happened, not a fresh marketing claim.
The process exposes what is not ready before it can affect the business.
Claims move forward only when the system can show what changed.
Lessons from one repo become reusable operating material for the next repo.

proof_summary.owner_translation: The repo shows origin, friction, proof, reuse, and story work as a path a reviewer can inspect.

safe_first_step: Start with one workflow map before broader access, automation, or claims.
sales_language_rules: Say manual work, follow-up, cash flow, owner approval, and customer experience before naming AI.
Treat commit counts as reviewer evidence, not hero copy.
Call unsupported claims blocked instead of softening them into promises.
Use slice to mean one bounded workflow improvement with a visible stop condition.
Make the next step feel safe: map first, then decide what earns access.
subhead: Map the manual route between tools, improve one bounded slice, prove what changed, and carry the lesson forward.

trust_answers.proof_behavior: The first slice must have a named boundary and stop condition.

trust_answers.owner_worry: I will not know what changed.
trust_answers.proof_behavior: Each claim links to evidence or stays blocked.
trust_answers.visible_answer: Every slice has a proof rail.

trust_answers.proof_behavior: Public proof is redacted, consented, generated, or replaced.
Unsupported claims show as blocked instead of becoming copy.

trust_answers.owner_worry: This will replace people before it understands the work.
trust_answers.proof_behavior: Human approval remains part of the workflow slice.
trust_answers.visible_answer: The first slice is small on purpose.

trust_answers.owner_worry: My tools already do not talk to each other.
trust_answers.proof_behavior: Integration starts with one workflow path, not the whole company.
trust_answers.visible_answer: The operating map starts with the disconnected tools the owner already uses.

trust_answers.proof_behavior: Runbooks, tests, receipts, and blockers sit behind the story.
trust_answers.visible_answer: The method is visible enough to inspect.

trust_answers.proof_behavior: Tool claims require current receipts before promotion.
trust_answers.visible_answer: Fast tools go through a controlled loop.

trust_answers.owner_worry: If it fails, I will be stuck.
trust_answers.proof_behavior: Failed proof does not become a public claim.
trust_answers.visible_answer: Stop conditions are named up front.

trust_answers.owner_worry: I do not want to become an AI expert.
trust_answers.proof_behavior: Technical proof is available but not required to understand the offer.
trust_answers.visible_answer: The owner approves the slice; the operator manages the machinery.

visual_direction.asset_rule: Use real workflow scenes, generated bitmap assets, or product-state visuals; avoid generic abstract gradients.
visual_direction.density_rule: Let the first screen feel alive, then let the buyer choose owner, method, proof, or reviewer depth.
visual_direction.first_impression: A working operating room for a real SMB workflow, not a floating SaaS card stack.
visual_direction.motion_rule: Use motion to show handoff, proof state, and lesson carry-forward; do not use decoration-only motion.
